app_summary: The app is a Reddit client named RedReader, which allows users to access content through the Reddit API. It features a main menu with options for 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', 'Find Location', 'Subscribed Subreddits', and 'Reddit Accounts'. The app includes a user agreement that must be accepted before accessing content. It also offers extensive settings for customizing the user experience, including appearance, font scaling, and behavior.

core_workflows.description: Step 1: The user opens the app and sees the User Agreement screen (State 0) which displays the RedReader terms. Step 2: The user taps the 'Accept' button at the bottom of the agreement dialog, navigating to the Reddit User Agreement confirmation (State 10). Step 3: The user taps 'I Agree' to confirm acceptance, which loads the Front Page (State 11) showing a list of subscribed subreddits like 'art', 'askreddit', and 'aww'.
core_workflows.purpose: First-time user onboarding flow that gates access behind agreement acceptance.

core_workflows.description: Step 1: The user opens the app and sees the User Agreement screen (State 0). Step 2: The user taps the 'Accept' button, navigating to the Reddit User Agreement confirmation (State 10). Step 3: The user taps the 'Close' button to return to the Pinned Subreddits screen (State 9). Step 4: The user taps the 'Back' button to return to the Reddit User Agreement screen (State 10).
core_workflows.purpose: Flow that allows users to navigate back and forth between the agreement and subreddit list screens.

screen_contexts.context: This screen shows the Reddit User Agreement for RedReader, an application that accesses content via the Reddit API. It prompts the user to read the terms and conditions from a provided link and confirm their acceptance.

The screen displays a Settings menu for an application, with options categorized under Appearance, Font, Behaviour, Images/Video, Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, and Backup/Restore. There is also an 'About' section.

The screen displays the main interface of the RedReader app, featuring a clock showing 11:30 at the top. It includes options for 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', and 'Find Location'. There is a section for 'Subscribed Subreddits' listing categories such as art, creepy, dataisbeautiful, diy, documentaries, and earthporn. A message indicates that the user is not logged in and provides an option to log in or remain anonymous.

The screen displays the main menu of the RedReader application with options for 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', 'Find Location', 'Subscribed Subreddits', and 'Reddit Accounts'. Under 'Reddit Accounts', there are options to add a Reddit account and view the current anonymous account which is active.

The screen shows a list of Pinned Subreddits with '/t/diy' highlighted. Below it, there is a list of Subscribed Subreddits including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.

The screen indicates that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted and prompts the user to accept the agreement to access content through the Reddit API. There are buttons to view the details and the agreement itself.

The screen shows a login prompt for RedReader, a Reddit client. It informs the user that they will be redirected to a web browser to log in to Reddit and then redirected back to the app. It also provides a link for help if the user encounters issues during the login process. The screen includes a list of some subreddits and two buttons: Close and Continue.

This screen displays a list of subreddits in RedReader, including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', 'documentaries', and 'earthporn'. It appears to be a general subreddit list screen, likely part of the main navigation of the app.

The screen shows a list of pinned subreddits in RedReader, with options to copy a link, view in an external browser, share, block the subreddit, and unpin from the main menu. The subreddits listed are 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.

The screen displays an error message indicating that the web page at https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact could not be loaded due to an internet disconnection. The error message is 'net::ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED'.

The screen shows a message indicating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. It prompts the user to accept the agreement to access content through the Reddit API. There are two buttons: 'DETAILS' and 'VIEW AGREEMENT'.

The screen displays the main menu of the RedReader app. It includes options for 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', and 'Find Location'. It also shows a pinned subreddit '/t/diy' and a list of subscribed subreddits including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', and 'reddit.com/r/diy'.
The screen displays an error message indicating that the web page at https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact could not be loaded due to an internet disconnection.

The screen shows the main menu of the RedReader app with options for 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', and 'Find Location'. It also lists pinned subreddits including 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.

The screen displays a list of subreddits in RedReader, including 'Pinned Subreddits' and 'Subscribed Subreddits'. The pinned subreddit '/t/diy' is highlighted, and the subscribed subreddits listed include 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.

The screen shows a message indicating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted, preventing access to content through the Reddit API. Options to view details and accept the agreement are provided.

The screen displays a list of subreddits in RedReader, including 'Pinned Subreddits' and 'Subscribed Subreddits'. The pinned subreddit '/t/diy' is highlighted, and the subscribed subreddits listed include 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.

The screen displays a message indicating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. It shows a timestamp of 11:31, a title 'Front Page', and a link to 'reddit.com/hot'. There is a button to 'VIEW AGREEMENT DETAILS' for users who wish to access content through the Reddit API.

The screen displays a settings menu with options for Reddit user agreement, past versions, submit post, accounts, themes, and settings. It also includes a search bar and a 'VIEW AGREEMENT' button related to the Reddit user agreement.
The screen shows a list of theme options for the application, including Night, Night (low contrast), Green, Blue, Light Blue, Orange, Gray, and Ultra Black. The current time is also displayed.

The screen displays font settings for various elements within the application, such as post titles, post subtitles, opened post titles, opened post subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. Each element can use the global font scale.

The screen displays settings related to font scaling. It includes a list of font scale options ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x, with 'Normal' being the default option. There are two headers at the top: 'Comment' and 'Inbox'. The screen also includes a 'Cancel' button for exiting the settings without making changes.

The screen is a settings page focused on font customization. It includes options to adjust the font scale for various elements such as global settings, post titles, post subtitles, opened post titles, opened post subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. Each element can either use the global font scale or have a specific scale, with the comment/inbox headers set to 0.7x.

The screen displays a settings menu related to subtitles and font scaling. It includes an option to 'Post subtitles' and a list of font scale options ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x, with 'Normal' as the default selection. There is a 'Cancel' button at the bottom.

The screen displays font settings for various elements of the application. It includes options for adjusting the font scale for post titles, post subtitles, opened post titles, opened post subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. The global font scale is set to 'Normal', and the post subtitles and comment/inbox headers have specific font scales set to 1.1x and 0.7x, respectively. Other elements are set to use the global font scale.

The screen displays a settings menu related to font scaling options. It includes a list of font scale options ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x, with 'Normal' as the default option. There is also a 'Cancel' button to exit the settings without making changes.
screen_contexts.verification_note: Low OCR coverage (29%). Context may contain inferred elements.

screen_contexts.context: The screen displays font scaling settings for various elements of the app. It includes options for global font scale, post titles, post subtitles, opened post titles, opened post subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. Each element has a specific scaling option, such as 1.1x for post subtitles and 1.4x for opened post titles.

This screen shows a Settings menu with categories for Appearance, Font, Behaviour, Images/Video, Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, Backup/Restore, and About. It appears to be a navigation menu for accessing different settings sections.
